description_long: This house features a traditional two-story structure with a prominent, steeply pitched gable roof covered in dark, weathered tiles. The exterior walls are finished in a faded terracotta color with visible patches of exposed stonework, giving it a rustic and aged appearance. The front facade includes a covered porch supported by four columns, with a set of stone steps leading up from a raised stone platform. Windows are small and symmetrically placed on the upper story, while the lower story is partially sheltered by the extended roof overhang. The house is surrounded by a low stone wall, creating a defined perimeter and entryway. The overall design suggests influences from East Asian vernacular architecture, particularly Japanese or Korean traditional houses.
description: A two-story house with a steep gable roof, terracotta and stone exterior, and a covered front porch on a raised stone platform. The structure is surrounded by a low stone wall and features small, symmetrically placed windows.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry with stone steps, a covered porch supported by columns, and two upper-story windows.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the steep gable roof, exposed stone patches on the wall, and the depth of the porch overhang.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying a plain wall with no visible windows or doors, emphasizing the stone and terracotta finish.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the gable roof, stone wall, and weathered exterior.
attribute_entryway_design: Central entry with stone steps, covered porch, four supporting columns, and extended roof overhang
